_comment: Canonical master schema for the GHIG9 12-month reporting tool, inferred from the real grantee files. This is the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH: edit it by hand to add indicators/aliases, change canonical names, mark columns as qualitative/quantitative/date, or retune thresholds — no .ts changes needed. NOTE: all row/column indices in sheet_roles are 1-BASED; parse.ts converts them to the 0-based grid that SheetJS produces.

outputs.canonical_name: Number of AI accuracy improvements deployed
Number of AI implementation evaluations conducted
Number of AI solutions integrated to the product
Number of data governance policies established for data sources
Number of new AI algorithms created
Number of new cloud/local structure established for data base management or AI deployment
Number of new data collected/acquired
Number of safeguards for AI accuracy established
Number of security measures established for data protections
Number of trainings/fine tunings of the AI tool conducted
Number of health workers equipped with job as, health education materials, or other support materials to improve the quality of healthcare services proved
Number of health workers trained in disease diagnosis, care, management and reporting (e.g., CHWs, Nurses, Trainer of Trainers, Health provers, Physicians, Pharmacists) for your target disease

Number of health workers trained in immunization and practices, such as vaccine usage and tracking, vaccine hesitancy, and proving patient/community vaccine education (e.g., CHWs, Nurses, Trainer of Trainers, Health provers, Physicians, Pharmacists)
Number of health workers trained in the AI platforms, tools, or technology (e.g., CHWs, Nurses, Trainer of Trainers, Health provers, Physicians, Pharmacists)
Number of job as, health worker education materials, or other support materials for staff created (on any topic)
Total number of health workers trained (should equal sum of rows 36 and 37)
Total number of unique clinical workers trained
Total number of unique non-clinical workers trained
Number of community awareness /education campaigns
Number of indivuals reached through community awareness /education campaigns (target audience of the campaigns)
Number of indivuals reached through other community awareness and education efforts covering prevention and care (eclude community campaigns or education during patients visits)
Number of patients educated during patient visits on prevention and care
Number of research publications, white papers, toolkits, policy papers, position papers, briefings, and other knowledge products supported by GHIG9 project

Number of existing points of care with expanded services. Expanded services means adding new healthcare service offerings to points of care, which are broadly defined as access points for receiving healthcare services.
Number of facilities with new/updated clinical practice protocols
Number of facilities with new/updated immunization micro-plans
Number of facilities with new/updated laboratory protocols
Number of facilities with new/updated supply chain management procedures
Number of facilities with the AI technology platform implemented
Total number of facilities strengthened through one or more of the changes below. Note that facilities in this section refers to brick-and-mortar facilities.
Number of new partnerships formed with points of care (for innovators who do not operate their own facilities or points of care)
Total number of new partnerships formed (including any partnerships with points of care)
Number of new care decision pathway established
Number of new healthcare access point established
Number of new points of care established
Number of pathways for new resource allocation established
Number of peri-urban areas operating in through GHIG9 project
Number of rural areas operating in through GHIG9 project
Number of urban areas operating in through GHIG9 project
Total number of sub-national geographies (districts/states/provinces) operating in through GHIG9 project
Total number of urban, peri-urban, and/ or rural areas operating in through GHIG9 project (should equal sum of rows 5, 6, and 7)
Number of Children Under 5 years receiving confirmed diagnosis of your target disease area
Number of Children Under 5 years screened for your target disease area

Number of Children Under 5 years with confirmed diagnosis for your target disease area receiving appropriate care or referred to an external facility for appropriate care (Appropriate care refers to services according to WHO guildeline or national clinical guidelines)
Number of FEMALE Children Under 5 years reached with direct healthcare services
Number of FEMALE patients reached with direct healthcare services for your target disease area
Number of MALE Children Under 5 years reached with direct healthcare services
Number of MALE patients reached with direct healthcare services for your target disease area
Number of people in catchment areas with access to GHIG-affiliated points of care
Number of pregnant women and new mothers receiving confirmed diagnosis of your target disease area

Number of pregnant women and new mothers receiving confirmed diagnosis of your target disease area, and receiving appropriate care (Appropriate care refers to services according to WHO guildeline or national clinical guelines)
Number of pregnant women and new mothers screened for your target disease area
Total number of Children Under 5 years reached with direct healthcare services
Total number of patients from LOW-INCOME HOUSEHOLDS reached with direct healthcare services for your target disease area (we use the international poverty line: $2.15 per person per day

Total number of patients from MARGINALIZED COMMUNITIES reached with direct healthcare services for your target disease area (Marginalized communities defined as communities that experience discrimination and exclusion (social, political and economic) because of unequal power relationships across economic, political, social and cultural dimensions)
Total number of patients reached with direct healthcare services for your target disease area
Number of children 0 to 5 years receiving vaccinations (including newborns)
Number of newborns (<1 month old) receiving vaccinations
Number of pregnant women and new mothers receiving recommended vaccinations (based on the EPI program in program country/ies)
Total number of indivuals receiving vaccinations (all sexes and ages)

outcomes.canonical_name: Percent of health and lab workers trained

Percent of healthcare workers with improved knowledge of infectious diseases (including childhood illnesses) AMR stewardship, maternal and newborn health, and cancer prevention screening, diagnosis screening, diagnosis, referral, treatment, and vaccination
Percent change in healthcare utilization in the community (demonstrated through increased immunization and/or care-seeking for IDs)
Percent change in average cost per patient served

Percent of patients/community members with improved knowledge of infectious diseases (including childhood illnesses), AMR stewardship, maternal and newborn health, and cancer prevention screening, diagnosis, referral, treatment, and vaccination
Changes in policies, budget allocations, endorsements or adoption of efforts that enable scale, sustainability or greater reach of quality healthcare for your target disease
Percent of Clinicians Adhering to Antimicrobial Stewardship Interventions

Percent of children aged 12-23 months that have received the last recommended dose for each vaccine in national schedule (children that are completely up to date and have received final doses of all recommended vaccines)
Percent of children that have received the last recommended dose (4th dose) of malaria vaccine
Percent of patients (all ages) that have received HPV vaccine
Percent of pregnant women attending at least four antenatal visits.
Percent of women screened for cancer
Percentage of people accessing the AI tool
Percent of children under 5 years given appropriate treatment and/or referral for childhood diseases
Percent of patients accessing care supported by the AI platform
